issues.title: Context freed before error check in sm4EncryptFinal
issues.problem: The context is freed (free(ctx)) before checking the return value of SDF_EncryptFinal. If the operation fails, the context is freed and then an exception is thrown. The Java side still has a reference to the freed context handle, which can lead to use-after-free or double-free vulnerabilities if cleanup code attempts to access the context again.

issues.title: Context freed before error check in sm4DecryptFinal
issues.problem: Same issue as sm4EncryptFinal - the context is freed (free(ctx)) before checking the return value of SDF_DecryptFinal. This can lead to use-after-free or double-free vulnerabilities if error handling code attempts to access the freed context.

issues.title: sm2Sign modifies caller's private key array
issues.problem: After memset(privKeyBytes, 0, ...) to clear sensitive data, ReleaseByteArrayElements is called with mode 0 which copies back to the original Java array. This writes zeros to the caller's original private key array, causing unexpected data corruption.

issues.title: sm2Decrypt modifies caller's private key array
issues.problem: Same issue as sm2Sign - after memset(privKeyBytes, 0, ...) to clear sensitive data, ReleaseByteArrayElements is called with mode 0 which copies the zeros back to the caller's original array.

issues.title: sm2Verify doesn't validate signature array length
issues.problem: The sm2Verify function checks publicKeyX and publicKeyY lengths but doesn't validate the signature array length. At lines 170-171, it copies 64 bytes (SM2_KEY_BYTES * 2) unconditionally from sigBytes, which can cause out-of-bounds reads if a shorter array is passed.

issues.title: sm2Decrypt copies 32 bytes from unchecked private-key array
issues.problem: sm2Decrypt gets privKeyLen but never validates it equals SM2_KEY_BYTES (32) before copying 32 bytes at line 298. Passing a shorter array causes out-of-bounds read.

issues.title: SM4 streaming init reads past short key or IV arrays
issues.problem: sm4EncryptInit and sm4DecryptInit copy a fixed 16-byte key and IV without checking array lengths first. At lines 259 and 415, memcpy reads SM4_IV_LENGTH (16) bytes without validation, causing out-of-bounds reads for undersized arrays.

issues.title: Memory leak in SM3MessageDigest.engineDigest
issues.problem: After calling sm3Final, the native context (allocated by sm3Init with malloc) is never freed. The ctx is set to 0 but sm3Free is never called, causing a memory leak for each digest operation. The sm3Free function exists but is only called in cleanupContext(), which is only invoked from engineReset() and finalize(), not from engineDigest().

issues.title: SM2PrivateKey exposes mutable private-key state
issues.problem: The constructor assigns the keyBytes reference directly without cloning, and getEncoded() returns the same internal array. This allows external code to modify the internal key state. Additionally, destroy() zeros the array which affects the caller's original array if they passed it directly to the constructor.

issues.title: Provider registration hard-fails without SDF library
issues.problem: The constructor immediately loads the native bridge and initializes the SDF device via ensureNativeLoaded(). This means simple provider discovery and registration fail on machines without hardware or a configured library. Users cannot even register the provider to check if it's available later.

issues.title: SM2 output-size calculation is off by one byte
issues.problem: The native code produces C1_X || C1_Y || C3 || C2 (96 bytes overhead), but engineGetOutputSize() assumes a nonexistent leading 0x04 byte and uses 97. For decryption, Cipher.getOutputSize() underestimates by one byte, causing potential ShortBufferException.

issues.title: SM2 cipher advertises unsupported PKCS1Padding
issues.problem: engineSetPadding() accepts "PKCS1Padding" but the implementation never applies any PKCS#1-style formatting. This is misleading - the method claims to support a padding mode it doesn't implement.

issues.title: DER signature parsing accepts malformed encodings
issues.problem: derToRaw() only rejects lengths that are too short (in.available() < length), not extra trailing data. The copyToFixedBuffer() method silently truncates oversized INTEGERs. This means malformed signatures are normalized instead of being rejected.

issues.title: sm3Free doesn't clear sensitive data before freeing
issues.problem: The sm3Free function frees the context without clearing potentially sensitive data. For consistency with sm4Free (which does memset before free at line 552), sm3Free should also clear the context before freeing.

issues.title: SM2PublicKey getX/getY return internal arrays without cloning
issues.problem: The getX() and getY() methods return the internal byte arrays directly without cloning, allowing external modification of the key's internal state.

issues.title: SM2ParameterSpec getUserId returns internal array without cloning
issues.problem: The getUserId() method returns the internal userId array directly without cloning, allowing external modification of the parameter's internal state.
